rows.notes: Top page title, clearly centered and without numbers. Script is legible and there are no monetary figures on this line.
Second-line heading under the title. Handwriting clearly reads as a locality (Civitati Oxon) with no amounts aligned to it.

Description partly uncertain (handwritten abbreviation). The three right-hand numerals align with this row as 0 (pounds), 1 (shilling), 4 (pence); the digits are fairly small and the text has some ambiguity in letters, so confidence is moderate.
Description uncertain by one or two letters ('Capony' interpreted from script). The amount 0–14–6 is clear and well aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Text is difficult to read (several abbreviated Latin/college names); amount 0–18–0 is clearly written and fits column alignment. Description is a best-effort reading of the abbreviation.
College name (Latin) reasonably legible. Amount 4–12–4 is distinct and the pounds figure (4) is clearly larger and aligned into the pounds column.
Name 'St Johan Bapt' (St John Baptist) is clearly written. Amount 0–12–6 is legible and properly aligned in the shillings and pence columns.

College name 'Baliol' is legible but faint. The amount appears as 1–19–0; the pounds digit is small and slightly ambiguous against nearby marks but aligns with the pounds column so moderately confident.
Description interpreted as 'Curiae Hospitij' (curia/hospital). Amount 1–14–8 is clearly written and aligned; handwriting for text is legible enough to make this reading plausible.
Long place/office description with abbreviations; the words 'Procurations' and 'Merton' are readable. Amount 1–10–4 is clear and well aligned.
Entry reads 'Janitorj' (janitor). Amount 2–0–0 is unambiguous and centered in the pounds column.
Description likely 'Coquo' (cook). Amount 0–13–4 is clearly visible in shillings/pence columns; text is somewhat stylized but readable.
Word is uncertain ('Lotricj' is a best-effort reading). The amount 0–13–4 is visible and aligns with the shilling/pence columns; text ambiguity reduces confidence.
Interpreted as 'Paupers' from handwriting. Amount 0–13–4 is clear and consistently aligned with prior rows of similar magnitude.
Reads as 'Bibliothecario' (librarian). Amount 1–3–0 is legible in the columns; the text is also reasonably clear in this row.
Text 'Bedle Begary' is a best interpretation of the script. Amount 1–6–8 is visible though the pence digit (8) has slight ink blotting; alignment supports this reading.
Occupation/office line (interpreted as 'Scultor & Quadrangl'). The amount 4–0–0 is large and clearly placed in the pounds column; the description letters are less distinct.
Handwriting for the title is unclear ('Horner Supt' is a likely reading). Amount 1–0–0 is clear but description uncertainty lowers confidence.
Entry reads as 'Conferation Sermon' (or 'Conferation / Sermon'); the amount 10–0–0 is very clear and prominent in the pounds column.
Interpreted as 'Curate Southw: Annu' or similar. Amount 0–2–6 is legible; text abbreviations cause moderate uncertainty.
Probably 'Com: Prices' or 'Corn Prices' (handwriting ambiguous). Amount 0–7–0 is clear in alignment. Chose 'Com. Prices' as a conservative reading of letters.
Long, abbreviated description; likely a synod/provincial charge for Southm Morton. Amount 0–10–0 is legible; text is uncertain due to abbreviations.
Two small bracketed items 'Castle' and 'Bocardo' written under a 'Prizes' heading. The combined amount 1–10–0 is readable; description is reconstructed from bracketed notes.
Latin form 'Pauperibus' is legible. Amount 0–2–0 is clear and well aligned.

There are two small follow-on lines for the rectory (both read as 0–2–0). The handwriting is cramped and slightly faded; the numbers 0–2–0 are still legible. Description is an interpretive summary of the two similar lines.
Bottom-line total reads 'Sum' with amount 35–12–6 clearly written and aligned. Shillings (12) and pence (6) are within valid ranges; ink is slightly faded but digits are unambiguous.
